title: myroxylon panamense
intro: Myroxylon panamense, also known as the Palo santo tree, is a tropical tree native to the dry forests of Central and South America, particularly in countries such as Panama, Mexico, and Ecuador. The resin extracted from the heartwood of this tree is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Palo santo include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve respiratory health, and protect against infections. Traditional herbal preparations involve distilling the resin into essential oils, which are then used in aromatherapy, as well as being used in perfumes and natural remedies for skin and respiratory issues.
